title: Invalid Character Validation in a String

statement.markdown: Given a string S of lowercase English alphabets, implement a function to validate if it contains all unique vowels. The function should return 0 for 'yes', 1 for 'no'. You are allowed to use only two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the string.
statement.inputFormat: A single string S of lowercase English alphabets.
statement.outputFormat: Return 0 if the string contains all unique vowels, 1 otherwise.
statement.notes: The input string is guaranteed to be at least 2 characters long and all its characters are lowercase English alphabets.

examples.explanation: The string 'aeiou' contains all unique vowels.
The string 'aio' does not contain all unique vowels

hints.text: Check if the string contains any vowel.
Create a hash set of vowels and check for uniqueness.

editorial.title: Invalid Character Validation Solution
editorial.intuition: We can use two pointers to traverse the string from start to end.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution would be to check each character in the string to see if it's a vowel.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a set to store unique vowels and then use two pointers to traverse the string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function will return 0 if the string contains all unique vowels and 1 otherwise.
editorial.dryRun: For input 'aeiou', the function will return 0.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int validateUniqueVowels(std::string S); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int validateUniqueVowels(String S); }
starterCode.python:
class Solution:
    def validateUniqueVowels(self, S: str) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int validateUniqueVowels(String S) { int unique = 0; java.util.HashSet<Character> vowels = new java.util.HashSet<Character>(); vowels.add('a'); vowels.add('e'); vowels.add('i'); vowels.add('o'); vowels.add('u'); for (int i = 0; i < S.length(); i++) if (vowels.contains(S.charAt(i))) if (i != S.length()-1 && vowels.contains(S.charAt(i+1))) unique = 1; return unique; } }
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function validateUniqueVowels(S) { let unique = 0; let vowels = new Set(); vowels.add('a'); vowels.add('e'); vowels.add('i'); vowels.add('o'); vowels.add('u'); for (let i = 0; i < S.length; i++) if (vowels.has(S[i])) if (i != S.length-1 && vowels.has(S[i+1])) unique = 1; return unique; }
